Done. Here's what was done:

**Root cause**: When real `featured_categories` are configured, the storefront templates (variants C, D, E) use `{% if cat.image %}...{% else %}🗂️{% endif %}` — so categories without an uploaded image all show the folder emoji.

**Fix**:
1. Added `icon = CharField(max_length=10)` to the `Category` model + migration applied
2. Updated variants C, D, E templates to check `cat.icon` first, then `cat.image`, then fall back to `🗂️`
3. Added an **Icon (Emoji)** field to the category add/edit form in the dashboard so you can set it per category

**What to do now**: Go to Dashboard → Categories → Edit each category and paste the emoji you want (📱, 💻, 🎧, etc.) into the new "Icon (Emoji)" field. It'll show immediately on the storefront.
You want me to set those icons directly in the database. Let me check what categories exist and update them:

input.__tool_use_purpose: Check existing categories and their current icons
input.command: python manage.py shell -c "from apps.products.models import Category; [print(c.pk, c.name, repr(c.icon)) for c in Category.objects.order_by('order','name')]"

content.data: Dashboard → Categories → click **Edit** on each category → there's now an **Icon (Emoji)** field at the top of the form. Paste the emoji there and save.

Done. Here's what was done:

1. **Set emojis** on all 8 categories in the DB: 📱 Phones, 💻 Laptops, 📲 Tablets, 🖥️ Monitors, ⌨️ Keyboards, 🏠 Smart Home, 🖨️ Printers, 🔧 Chipsets

2. **Dashboard editor** — `cat_products_json` now includes `icon`, so the editor preview cards and settings panel all show the emoji next to the category name

3. **Storefront templates** — all 4 variants that show real category data (A, C, D, E, F) now use `cat.icon` instead of the hardcoded `🗂️` fallback
Glad it's all working! When you're ready to tackle Tile B, just say the word.

Confirmed. Here's what we're implementing:

**Featured Products section in the homepage editor** — same pattern as Hero and Categories:

**Left panel**: "Featured Products" row in the sections list (already exists as a `HomepageSection` with key `featured`)

**Middle panel**: 3 preview cards (V1, V2, V3) showing scaled-down live previews using real product data from the DB

**Right panel**: When `activeKey === 'featured'`, shows:
- Section overline / heading / subheading text + colour controls
- Background colour picker
- Product selector — pick which products appear (from existing products in DB)
- "View All" link toggle (V1, V2 only)
- Save button → saves to `HomepageSection.custom_content`

**Storefront**: 3 template files (`featured_v1.html`, `featured_v2.html`, `featured_v3.html`) rendered by the existing `home_view` based on the active variant

**State**: `fv1`, `fv2`, `fv3` objects in `heroEditor()` (same Alpine component), `featVariant` tracks active variant, `featProducts` holds the selected product IDs loaded from saved state

**Data flow**: Dashboard view passes `products_json` (all visible products) to the editor for the product picker, and `featured_products_json` (saved selections) for the preview

Ready to proceed?

All done. System check passes clean. Here's a summary of what was built:

**Dashboard editor:**
- Featured Products section in the left nav activates the middle/right panels
- 3 scaled preview cards (A Grid, B Hero+Grid, C Scroll) with live product previews
- Right panel: overline, heading (with colour + size), subheading, background colour, product picker (searchable checkboxes, max 8/5/8 per variant), view all link
- Save button routes to `saveFeat()` which POSTs to the existing `homepage_section_save` endpoint

**Storefront:**
- `featured_v1.html` — 4-column grid with overline/heading/subheading/view-all
- `featured_v2.html` — hero card left + 2×2 grid right
- `featured_v3.html` — horizontal scroll, no view-all
- Products come from saved IDs in `custom_content`, falling back to `is_featured=True` products if none saved

All mutations now go through `setFv(key, val)` which does `this[k] = {...this[k], [key]: val}` — a full object replacement that Alpine's reactivity system will detect, updating the preview in real time.

Now the storefront also needs to use the section's `bg_colour` — let me check how `home.html` applies it:

The `home.html` loop already does `style="{{ section.get_bg_style }} {{ section.get_css_vars }}"` on the `<section>` tag, and `get_bg_style` uses `section.bg_colour`. So once `bg_colour` is saved to the section record, the storefront will pick it up automatically. Done.
Now try saving again from the editor and share the log line that shows `[SECTION SAVE]`. That will tell us exactly what value is being received.
Done. Both subheading and view all link now have colour pickers in the editor, the colours are saved in `custom_content`, and the storefront templates apply them via inline `style="color:var(...)"`.

Done. Summary of fixes:

- **Real-time updates**: All colour/text bindings in V2 and V3 previews now use reactive `:style` bindings referencing `fv2.xxx` / `fv3.xxx` directly (which update when `setFv` replaces the object)
- **Product card appearance**: V1 now uses inline styles matching the storefront card look (white card, rounded corners, shadow, image area, name + price)
- **Subheading/view-all colours**: All three previews now bind those colours reactively
Selected products will now always appear at the top of the list, with unselected ones below. The sort is reactive — as you check/uncheck items they reorder immediately.

`align-items:stretch` on the outer grid makes both columns equal height. The hero card uses `flex-direction:column` with `flex:1` on the image area so it expands to fill whatever height the 2×2 grid dictates.

In the **editor preview** (middle panel), the hero product in V2 is a custom inline div — not a reusable component. It's built directly in the template with Alpine bindings.

On the **storefront** (`featured_v2.html`), it uses `{% include "store/_product_card.html" %}` — the same `pc-card` component used everywhere else on the site.

The `.feat-hero-card` class scopes all overrides — the image area gets `min-height:260px` and `flex:1` so it fills the card height, the card itself stretches to match the right grid's height via `align-items:stretch`, and the name/price text is larger. Adjust `min-height:260px` to whatever looks right for your content.

The key change: `.feat-hero-card { height:100% }` makes the wrapper div fill the grid cell height, then `.pc-card { height:100% }` fills the wrapper, and `.pc-image-wrap { flex:1 }` makes the image expand to consume all remaining space after the body content. No fixed `min-height` — it naturally matches whatever height the 2×2 grid produces.
Done.

The storefront template (`featured_v2.html`) already uses `feat_products.0` as the hero — so by reordering the list in the view, the hero product automatically appears first with no template changes needed.

What this does:
- Removes the selected product from wherever it is in the list
- Unshifts it to position 0
- Trims to max 5 (variant B limit)
- Both `hero_product_id` and `product_ids` update reactively via `setFv`, so the preview updates immediately

Both `hero_product_id` and `product_ids` are now set in a single object replacement, so they're always in sync. After saving, the store view reads `product_ids[0]` as the hero product since `feat_hero_id` reorders the list to put it first.

`hero_product_id` is now prepended to the fetch list using `dict.fromkeys` to deduplicate while preserving order. The hero product will always be fetched and placed first, regardless of whether it's also in `product_ids`.

The key change: `overflow:hidden` on `pc-image-wrap` clips the image to the available flex space rather than letting it push the card taller. The image uses `object-fit:cover` so it fills the space without distortion regardless of the image's natural dimensions.

The `!important` on `height:auto` overrides the `height:160px` from `base.html`. With `overflow:hidden` on both the card and the image wrap, the image is clipped to whatever space `flex:1` allocates — it can't push the card taller than the grid height.

The key fix: `height:0 !important` on `pc-image-wrap` combined with `flex:1` and `min-height:0`. This is the standard CSS trick for flex children with images — setting `height:0` forces the flex algorithm to control the height via `flex:1` rather than letting the image's natural dimensions dictate it. The `overflow:hidden` then clips the image to that flex-allocated height.
Share the browser console logs and I'll diagnose from there.

Which part do you want logged? The hero card height issue is a pure CSS problem — no JS involved. Do you want:

1. **Server-side logs** — log what `feat_products_list` contains and the hero product when the homepage renders
2. **Client-side logs** — log the computed height of the hero card and the right grid after the page loads

Or both?
Refresh the homepage and share what the console shows for those 4 values.

Now the outer grid column uses `align-items:stretch` (already set), so `.feat-hero-card` stretches to match the right column's height. The card inside uses `flex:1` to fill that wrapper. The image wrap uses `flex:1; height:0 !important; min-height:0` — the `height:0` breaks the circular dependency by telling the flex algorithm to ignore the image's natural height and allocate space purely by flex ratio.

Check the logs again — the hero wrapper should now equal the right grid height.

The right grid is 630px total (2 cards). Each card has a 160px image + ~155px body = ~315px. The hero image at `420px` gives it a taller image area than the small cards while keeping the total card height close to the right column. Adjust `420px` up or down to taste — share the logs again and I can dial it in precisely.

As for `vh` — you could use something like `height:55vh` instead of a fixed pixel value, which scales with the viewport. But since the right grid height is fixed by the `pc-card` content (not viewport-dependent), `px` is more reliable here. `vh` would make the image taller on large screens and shorter on small ones, potentially mismatching the right grid on different screen sizes.
Clean.
Done. The dropdown now only shows products that are checked in the Products list.

Now the preview is fully reactive — no nested `x-data`, all bindings reference `fv2` directly so `setFv` replacements trigger re-renders. The hero product is excluded from the 2×2 grid, and unchecking a product clears it as hero if it was selected.

This ensures `fv1`, `fv2`, `fv3` are set via property assignment (`this[k] = ...`) during `init()`, which Alpine's reactivity system tracks. After that, `setFv` does the same — full replacement — so all subsequent changes will trigger re-renders.

Now reload the editor and share:
1. The `[feat init]` logs on page load
2. The `[setFv]` log when you click a colour swatch or check a product

These are server logs — I need the **browser console** logs. Open Chrome DevTools (F12) → Console tab, reload the editor page, then share what you see there (the `[feat init]` and `[setFv]` messages).
Now reload the editor and check the browser console — you should see `[feat init]` logs on load and `[setFv]` or the IIFE executing when you interact.
Now reload and check the console — you should see `[toggleFeatProduct]` and `[setFeatHero]` logs when interacting.
All debug logs removed.

Good — deals section has no editor support yet. The existing `deals_v1.html` uses `deals` (auto-fetched sale products) and `settings.section_heading_deals`. 

Here's the plan for Variant 1:

**What already exists and works:**
- `deals_v1.html` renders dark cards on navy background ✓
- `deals` queryset (sale products) passed from store view ✓
- Section pk=4, variant=1, bg_colour=--navy ✓

**What needs to be added:**
1. **Editor support** — add `deals` to `activeKey` routing in the middle/right panels, with controls for: heading, heading colour/size, product picker (same pattern as featured), "See All" link + colour, background colour
2. **Store view** — read saved `product_ids` from `deals` section `custom_content`, same pattern as featured
3. **`deals_v1.html`** — update to use editor-controlled heading and product list instead of `settings.section_heading_deals`

The existing dark card styling (`deal-card` class) already gives white text + gold prices — that's already correct for Variant 1.

Want me to proceed with this approach?

Variant 1 is **not** fully there yet — it uses `settings.section_heading_deals` (a static field) and auto-fetches all sale products. There's no editor panel to pick specific products, change the heading colour/size, or control the "See All" link. It just works by default.

For **Variant 2 — Countdown banner + grid**, the user needs to:

1. **Set a countdown end date/time** — a datetime picker in the editor right panel
2. **Optionally customise the banner text** — e.g. "Flash Sale ends in:"
3. Everything else (heading, products, colours) is the same as V1

The countdown timer itself is Alpine.js driven on the storefront — no server interaction needed after the end date is saved.

Should I:
- First wire up the editor for V1 (product picker, heading controls) so both variants share the same base
- Then add V2 on top with just the countdown date field

Or skip V1 editor wiring and go straight to V2 since V1 "works" as-is?
Done. The deals section now has a customisable subheading with its own colour picker, wired through the editor, store view, and storefront template.

It already is — I just added it. The right panel has a **Subheading** group with a text input and colour swatches, saved via `saveDeals()`, and rendered on the storefront via `deals_subheading` and `deals_subheading_colour`.

Are you not seeing it in the editor? If so, try a hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R).
Wrapping in a `<div>` makes the heading and subheading stack vertically as a block, while the flex container still works for any future right-side element (like a "See All" link in the header row).
Done. Subheading now has S/M/L/XL size options (13/16/18/22px) alongside the colour picker.
Reload the editor, go to Deals section, change the heading text, and share what the browser console shows.
Since `dv1` is confirmed as a Proxy, all the `:style` bindings referencing `dv1.xxx` should now update reactively. The subheading size was the only hardcoded value — that's now fixed.
On to Variant 2 (Countdown banner + grid) when you're ready.

Here's what Variant 2 adds on top of V1:

**Countdown banner** (above the grid):
- 🔥 icon + customisable banner label (e.g. "Flash Sale ends in:")
- Live countdown: `DD : HH : MM : SS` with labels
- Banner background colour (default `--emerald`)
- Banner text colour
- Auto-hides the countdown when expired (section stays visible, just no timer)

**Editor controls (right panel, V2 only):**
- Sale end date/time picker (`<input type="datetime-local">`)
- Banner label text
- Banner background colour
- Banner text colour

**Everything else** (heading, subheading, products, see all link) is identical to V1.

**Storefront:** Alpine.js `setInterval` drives the countdown — no server calls after page load. End date stored in `custom_content`.

What it does NOT do:
- No server-side enforcement (products don't auto-hide after expiry)
- No timezone conversion (uses browser local time)

Approve?

Confirmed. Updated spec for V2:

**Banner block (above grid):**
- Background colour (picker)
- Text colour (picker)
- "Flash sale" label — editable text + font size (S/M/L/XL) + toggle to show/hide
- Countdown timer — end datetime picker + font size (S/M/L/XL) + toggle to show/hide
- Auto-hides timer when expired

**Section content (same as V1 + extras):**
- Heading + colour + size
- Subheading + colour + size
- Products picker (max 8)
- See All link + colour

**Editor right panel shows all of the above when `dealsVariant==='2'`**

**State stored in `dv2` object in `custom_content.deal_variants`**

Approve?

Done. Summary:

- **Editor**: V2 preview card shows the banner + countdown placeholder + grid. Right panel shows banner/timer controls only when V2 is selected; heading/subheading/products/see-all are shared with V1.
- **Storefront** (`deals_v2.html`): Alpine `dealCountdown()` drives the live timer, auto-hides when expired. All text/colours/sizes come from the editor.
- **`deals_v1.html`** unchanged.
That restores the missing `<div class="ep-group">` wrapper for the Heading group, fixing the div imbalance that was pushing the toast outside the `x-data` scope.
The banner is now a single right-aligned flex row: label on the left of the timer group, all pushed to the right edge of the container.

import json as _json
    cat_saved_variants = cat_cc.get('cat_variants', {})
    featured_categories = settings.featured_categories.annotate(
        product_count=Count('products', filter=Q(products__is_visible=True))
    ).all()
    cat_products_json = _json.dumps([
        {'name': c.name, 'slug': c.slug, 'product_count': c.product_count or 0,
         'image_url': c.image.url if c.image else ''}
        for c in featured_categories
    ])
    deal_products = list(Product.objects.filter(is_visible=True, sale_price__isnull=False).prefetch_related('images').values(
        'id', 'name', 'base_price', 'sale_price'
    ).order_by('name')[:50])
    # Add cover image url per product
    from apps.products.models import ProductImage
    cover_map = {pi.product_id: pi.image.url for pi in ProductImage.objects.filter(
        product_id__in=[p['id'] for p in deal_products], is_cover=True
    )}
    for p in deal_products:
        p['cover_image_url'] = cover_map.get(p['id'], '')
        p['effective_price'] = float(p['sale_price'] if p['sale_price'] else p['base_price'])
        p['base_price'] = float(p['base_price'])
        p['sale_price'] = float(p['sale_price']) if p['sale_price'] else None
    import json as _json
    deal = deal_products[0] if deal_products else None

content.data:
name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    slug = models.SlugField(max_length=120, unique=True)
    description = models.TextField(blank=True)
    image = models.ImageField(upload_to='categories/', null=True, blank=True)
    parent = models.ForeignKey('self', null=True, blank=True, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, related_name='children')
    order = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)

    class Meta:
        ordering = ['order', 'name']
        verbose_name_plural = 'categories'

    def __str__(self):
        return self.name


class Product(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    slug = models.SlugField(max_length=220, unique=True)
    category = models.ForeignKey(Category, null=True, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, related_name='products')
    description = models.TextField()
    specs = models.TextField(blank=True)
    base_price = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2, default=0)
    sale_price = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2, null=True, blank=True)
    sku = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True)
    is_visible = models.BooleanField(default=True)
    is_featured = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    delivery_fee = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2, default=0)
    deal_end_date = models.DateTimeField(null=True, blank=True)
    stock = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=0)
    low_stock_threshold = models.PositiveIntegerField(default=5)

Now I understand the full picture. The `HomepageSection` model has no `icon` field, and the left nav just shows `section.label`. The user is saying that in the **category settings panel** (the right panel), the category names used to show with SVG icons or emojis next to them, but now they just show a folder icon.

This is almost certainly a browser rendering issue — the emojis in the category names (like 📱 Phones, 💻 Laptops) are being replaced by a folder icon, which typically means the emoji font isn't loading or the characters are being stripped.

Wait — let me re-read the user's message: "my cool svg icons and emojis disappeared and have been replaced with a folder icon across all categories". This sounds like the category `image` field — when a category has an image, it shows the image; when it doesn't, it shows a folder icon as a placeholder. content.data:
Now I see the issue clearly. In variants C (v3), D (v4), and E (v5), when `featured_categories` is set (i.e. real categories are configured), the template uses `{% if cat.image %}...{% else %}🗂️{% endif %}`. So if your real categories don't have images uploaded, they all show the `🗂️` folder emoji.

The fix is to add an `icon` field to the Category model, or more practically, to add an `emoji` field that can be set per category. But the simpler approach — since the Category model only has `image` — is to check if the view is passing `cat.icon` or `cat.emoji` from somewhere.

Let me check the view to see what's attached to each category:

# Get category_settings for variant B
    cat_settings = _cat_cv.get('category_settings', [])
    
    if featured_cats:
        for idx, cat in enumerate(featured_cats):
            if idx in visible_cats:
                colors = cat_colors_list[idx] if idx < len(cat_colors_list) else default_colors[idx % 6]
                cat_setting = cat_settings[idx] if idx < len(cat_settings) else {}
                cat_dict = {
                    'id': cat.id,
                    'name': cat.name,
                    'slug': cat.slug,
                    'image': cat.image,
                    'product_count': cat.product_count,
                    'border_color': colors.get('bg', '--navy'),
                    'text_color': colors.get('text', '--surface'),
                    # Variant B settings
                    'bg_image': cat_setting.get('bg_image'),
                    'bg_color': cat_setting.get('bg_color', '--navy'),
                    'overlay_color': cat_setting.get('overlay_color', '--navy'),
                    'overlay_opacity': cat_setting.get('overlay_opacity', 65),
                    'settings_text_color': cat_setting.get('text_color', '--surface'),
                }
                featured_cats_with_colors.append(cat_dict)

    return render(request, 'store/home.html', {
        'settings': settings,
        'sections': sections,
        'featured_products': featured,
        'deals': deals,
        'hero_deals': hero_deals,

        'best_sellers': best_sellers,

        'testimonials': testimonials,
        'cat_overline': _cat_cv.get('overline', 'Explore'),
        'cat_heading': _cat_cv.get('heading', 'Shop by Category'),
        'cat_heading_size': _cat_cv.get('heading_size', '38px'),
        'cat_heading_colour': _cat_cv.get('heading_colour', '--navy'),

    q = params.get('q', '').strip()

Now I have the full picture. The issue is clear:

In the storefront templates (v3, v4, v5), when real `featured_categories` are set, the icon slot shows `{% if cat.image %}...{% else %}🗂️{% endif %}`. Your categories don't have images uploaded, so they all show the `🗂️` folder emoji.

Previously, the categories were probably showing the placeholder emojis (📱, 💻, 🎧, etc.) because `featured_categories` was empty/not configured, so the hardcoded fallback blocks with those emojis were rendering. Now that you have real categories configured, it uses the real data path — which falls back to `🗂️` when no image is set.

The fix is to add an `icon` (emoji) field to the Category model so each category can have its own emoji icon. Let me implement this:

Done. Here's what was done:

**Root cause**: When real `featured_categories` are configured, the storefront templates (variants C, D, E) use `{% if cat.image %}...{% else %}🗂️{% endif %}` — so categories without an uploaded image all show the folder emoji.

**Fix**:
1. Added `icon = CharField(max_length=10)` to the `Category` model + migration applied
2. Updated variants C, D, E templates to check `cat.icon` first, then `cat.image`, then fall back to `🗂️`
3. Added an **Icon (Emoji)** field to the category add/edit form in the dashboard so you can set it per category

**What to do now**: Go to Dashboard → Categories → Edit each category and paste the emoji you want (📱, 💻, 🎧, etc.) into the new "Icon (Emoji)" field. It'll show immediately on the storefront.

**State**: `fv1`, `fv2`, `fv3` objects in `heroEditor()` (same Alpine component), `featVariant` tracks active variant, `featProducts` holds the selected product IDs loaded from saved state

**Data flow**: Dashboard view passes `products_json` (all visible products) to the editor for the product picker, and `featured_products_json` (saved selections) for the preview

class StoreSettings(models.Model):

    store_name = models.CharField(max_length=200, default='Mutisya')

    notif_low_stock = models.BooleanField(default=True)

    hero_subheading = models.CharField(max_length=300, blank=True)

    hero_cta2_text = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True)
    hero_cta2_link = models.CharField(max_length=200, blank=True)
    hero_image = models.ImageField(upload_to='store/', null=True, blank=True)
    # Story
    story_heading = models.CharField(max_length=120, blank=True)
    story_text = models.TextField(blank=True)
    story_cta_text = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True)
    story_cta_link = models.CharField(max_length=200, blank=True)
    story_image = models.ImageField(upload_to='store/', null=True, blank=True)

    section_newsletter_visible = models.BooleanField(default=True)
    # Section headings

    section_subheading_featured = models.CharField(max_length=200, blank=True)
    section_heading_deals = models.CharField(max_length=100, default="Today's Deals")

    section_heading_newsletter = models.CharField(max_length=100, default='Stay in the Loop')

content.data: The binding looks correct. The issue is that `fv1.bg_colour` defaults to `'--surface'` which is `#fff` — so it appears to not change because white-on-white is invisible, OR the swatch click is updating `afv.bg_colour` but `afv` returns `fv1` only when `featVariant==='1'`. content.data:
The problem is `afv.bg_colour=sw.token` — `afv` is a getter returning `this['fv'+this.featVariant]`, which is an object reference. Assigning a property on it should work... but Alpine's reactivity won't detect the mutation on a nested object property accessed via a getter. The same pattern works for `acv` in categories — let me check how that's done:

The categories use `acv.bg_colour=sw.token` too and it works. The difference is that `cv1`-`cv6` are initialized via `_cvDefaults()` in `init()` which creates full objects, while `fv1`/`fv2`/`fv3` are initialized as **inline object literals in the return statement** — Alpine evaluates these before `init()` runs, so they're plain objects. That should be fine.

The real issue: the preview card uses `:style="'background:var('+fv1.bg_colour+')...'"` — it references `fv1` directly, not `afv`. When you click a swatch it sets `afv.bg_colour` which mutates `fv1.bg_colour` (same reference), but Alpine may not detect the deep mutation. The fix is to force reactivity by reassigning the whole object:

The cleanest fix is to replace the `afv` getter with a method that does a reactive reassignment, or use a helper. ```css
.feat-hero-card .pc-image-wrap { flex:1; height:auto !important; overflow:hidden; }

`flex:1` makes the image wrap grow, but the `pc-card` itself has `overflow:hidden` — yet the card's height is `100%` of `.feat-hero-card` which is `92%` of the grid cell. The grid cell height is determined by the right column (2×2 grid of `pc-card`s), each with `height:160px` image wrap. So the right column is roughly `2 × (160px image + ~100px body + 16px gap) ≈ 452px`. The hero card at `92%` = ~416px. That should work.

The hero card is 962px but the right grid is only 630px. The `92%` is being calculated against the wrong parent — `.feat-hero-card` is `height:92%` of the grid cell, but the grid cell height is being set by the hero card itself (circular). The `align-items:stretch` outer grid means both columns try to match each other, but the hero card's content is driving the height up.

The fix: don't use percentage height at all. Use CSS grid on the outer container and let the right column define the row height, with the hero card filling it via `align-self:stretch`:

Now the outer grid column uses `align-items:stretch` (already set), so `.feat-hero-card` stretches to match the right column's height. The card inside uses `flex:1` to fill that wrapper. The image wrap uses `flex:1; height:0 !important; min-height:0` — the `height:0` breaks the circular dependency by telling the flex algorithm to ignore the image's natural height and allocate space purely by flex ratio.

Check the logs again — the hero wrapper should now equal the right grid height.

The hero is still driving the height — 1045px vs 630px right grid. The circular dependency is still there because `align-items:stretch` makes both columns match the *tallest* one, which is the hero.

The only way to break this is to give the outer grid an explicit height equal to the right column. The right column is 630px — so set `grid-template-rows` or cap the outer grid height. But we don't know 630px at CSS time.

The IIFE with `.call(this)` — in Alpine's expression context, `this` inside an IIFE doesn't refer to the Alpine component. That's why nothing happens.

Good — deals section has no editor support yet. The existing `deals_v1.html` uses `deals` (auto-fetched sale products) and `settings.section_heading_deals`. 

Here's the plan for Variant 1:

**What already exists and works:**
- `deals_v1.html` renders dark cards on navy background ✓
- `deals` queryset (sale products) passed from store view ✓
- Section pk=4, variant=1, bg_colour=--navy ✓

**What needs to be added:**
1. **Editor support** — add `deals` to `activeKey` routing in the middle/right panels, with controls for: heading, heading colour/size, product picker (same pattern as featured), "See All" link + colour, background colour
2. **Store view** — read saved `product_ids` from `deals` section `custom_content`, same pattern as featured
3. **`deals_v1.html`** — update to use editor-controlled heading and product list instead of `settings.section_heading_deals`

The existing dark card styling (`deal-card` class) already gives white text + gold prices — that's already correct for Variant 1.

Want me to proceed with this approach?
if it is already there for 1, lets go on with 2, what does the user need to o

Variant 1 is **not** fully there yet — it uses `settings.section_heading_deals` (a static field) and auto-fetches all sale products. There's no editor panel to pick specific products, change the heading colour/size, or control the "See All" link. It just works by default.

For **Variant 2 — Countdown banner + grid**, the user needs to:

1. **Set a countdown end date/time** — a datetime picker in the editor right panel
2. **Optionally customise the banner text** — e.g. "Flash Sale ends in:"
3. Everything else (heading, products, colours) is the same as V1

The countdown timer itself is Alpine.js driven on the storefront — no server interaction needed after the end date is saved.

Should I:
- First wire up the editor for V1 (product picker, heading controls) so both variants share the same base
- Then add V2 on top with just the countdown date field

Or skip V1 editor wiring and go straight to V2 since V1 "works" as-is?
